sampled.snippet: Parallel imaging (PI) [1][2][3] forms the foundation of accelerated data acquisition in magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), which is tremendously time-consuming. In the last decade, PI combined with compressed sensing (CS) techniques have resulted in substantial improvements in acquisition speed and image quality.

observed.at_claimed_location: Parallel imaging (PI)  [1] [2] [3]  forms the foundation of accelerated data acquisition in magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), which is tremendously time-consuming. In the last decade, PI combined with compressed sensing (CS) techniques have resulted in substantial improvements in acquisition speed and image quality.  [4] [5] [6] [7] [8] [9]  Although PI-CS can achieve state-of-the-art performance, designing effective regularization schemes and tuning of hyper-parameters are not trivial. Starting in 2016, deep learning algorithms have become extremely popular and effective tools in data-driven learning of inverse problems and have enabled progress beyond the limitations of CS.
observed.note: Snippet matches line 1 of _ INTRODUCTION.txt verbatim except for trivial whitespace around bracketed citation markers ([1][2][3] vs [1] [2] [3]). Both clauses (PI foundation + PI+CS substantial improvements) are present exactly as recorded.
